org:2060: Technology company building decentralized identity, secure messaging, and interoperable wallet products based on open standards.
org:amsterdam: Municipality of Amsterdam; runs digital identity and wallet pilots with residents and businesses, including Yivi-based demos.
org:aruba: Italian cloud, certified email (PEC), and digital trust provider (Aruba Group) serving businesses and the public sector across Europe.
org:austria: Austrian e-government and digital trust ecosystem; includes A-SIT Plus open-source wallet components and national ID technology.
org:ayanworks: Indian software company behind Credebl, an open-source stack for verifiable credentials and decentralized identity.
org:belgium: Federal government of Belgium; provides national portals, e-government services, and digital identity programmes for citizens and businesses.
org:brazil: Brazilian Ministry of Management and Innovation in Public Services (MGI), driving modernization and digital public services nationwide.
org:cyprus: Government of Cyprus; national digital government services, online portals, and programmes for citizens and residents.
org:danubetech: Austrian company delivering standards-based decentralized identity, wallets, and interoperability software for enterprises and governments.
org:digital-trust-solutions: Dutch consultancy helping organizations design and implement digital trust, identity, and wallet solutions.
org:digitalbazaar: US technology company advancing decentralized identifiers, verifiable credentials, and secure data ecosystems (including JSON-LD VC work).
org:estonia: Estonian public sector digital governance; the Information System Authority (RIA) operates national e-ID, trust services, and cyber resilience.
org:eudiplo: OpenWallet Foundation lab initiative building reference tooling for an EU Digital Identity Wallet oriented to diplomatic and institutional use cases.
org:ewc: European Wallet Consortium (EWC); research network on interoperable digital wallets, coordinated from the University of the Aegean.
org:finland: Finnish public administration; DVV leads population and identity services and pilots the European Digital Identity Wallet in Finland.
org:germany: German innovation and government programmes on the EU Digital Identity Wallet, including SPRIND-backed prototyping and ecosystem coordination.
org:google: Global technology company offering cloud, Android, and developer platforms; participates in industry and standards work on identity and credentials.
org:greece: Greek government digital programmes, including the national digital wallet initiative (wallet.gov.gr) for citizens.
org:helixid: German company (Blockchain HELIX AG) providing decentralized identity and verifiable credential solutions for regulated industries.
org:honduras: National Registry of Persons (RNP Honduras); maintains civil registration and national identification for the Republic of Honduras.
org:inetum: European IT services and digital solutions group supporting public sector and enterprise transformation across multiple countries.
org:innotractor: Technology firm delivering software and integration services for secure digital identity, authentication, and data exchange.
org:k4security: German cybersecurity company offering consulting, managed security, and identity-related protection for organizations.
org:latvia: Republic of Latvia government services; national digital portals and programmes for identity, e-services, and public administration.
org:minisuomi: Finnish demonstration wallet environment (“Mini-Suomi”) operated by DVV to trial digital identity and wallet scenarios.
org:moldova: Moldova e-Government Agency; coordinates national digital transformation, online public services, and digital identity infrastructure.
org:mydata-global: International nonprofit promoting human-centric personal data control, interoperability, and ethical data use across sectors.
org:neoke: Company building identity orchestration and wallet connectivity products for enterprises and regulated ecosystems.
org:northernblock: Canadian software company focused on digital identity wallets, verifiable credentials, and enterprise-grade issuance tooling.
org:owf: OpenWallet Foundation; open-governance community developing specifications and reference implementations for interoperable digital wallets.
org:poland: Polish government digital services; mObywatel is the national mobile app for official documents, notifications, and e-government features.
org:portugal: Portuguese government e-government portal (ePortugal) aggregating digital public services for citizens and businesses.
org:privacybydesign: Privacy by Design Foundation; steward of Yivi (formerly IRMA), an attribute-based privacy-preserving identity wallet and issuer ecosystem.
org:slovenia: Government of Slovenia; national portals and digital programmes for public administration, identity, and e-services.
org:spherity: German company providing decentralized identity and digital twin credentials for automotive, industry, and regulated supply chains.
org:sweden: Swedish Agency for Digital Government (DIGG); drives digital transformation, interoperability, and e-government for agencies and citizens.
org:thales: Multinational group delivering aerospace, transport, defence, and digital identity/security solutions including government ID programmes.
org:uaegean: Public university in Greece; hosts research groups working on digital wallets, security, and EU-funded identity projects (e.g. DSS).
